@@ -1063,7 +1063,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8","title":"Rollout track: governance, security/privacy, migration, and operations docs","description":"## Track objective
Operationalize the feature safely: feature flags, migration/rollback, security/privacy controls, runbooks, and user/operator documentation.

## Why this track exists
A high-throughput recorder can become a high-risk subsystem without explicit governance on retention, privacy, cost, and rollback.

## Deliverables
- Phased rollout plan with canary and kill-switch controls
- Security/privacy model (redaction, access boundaries, retention)
- Migration and rollback playbooks
- End-user and operator docs with troubleshooting workflows

## Acceptance criteria
- Production rollout can be paused/reverted without data ambiguity.
- Ops docs are sufficient for independent on-call execution.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.770578Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.118202Z","labels":["docs","ops","privacy","rollout","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.770578Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.077136Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.118153Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","title":"Define staged feature-flag rollout plan with explicit go/no-go/rollback criteria","description":"## Context
Recorder/search rollout must be staged behind feature flags with clear go/no-go criteria to reduce operational risk.

## Scope
Define phased rollout plan (off -> shadow -> limited -> default-on) with explicit guardrails and rollback triggers.

## Deliverables
- Feature flags and config defaults per phase
- Exit criteria and rollback triggers for each phase
- Stakeholder communication checklist for phase changes
- Decision log template for rollout approvals

## Why this matters
Controlled rollout avoids destabilizing core ft workflows while introducing deep new infrastructure.

## Acceptance
- Every phase has measurable entry/exit criteria
- Rollback can be executed quickly and safely
- Flags are documented and testable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.501988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.736785Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","governance","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.501988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.736721Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","title":"Plan and validate migration path from existing capture/search to recorder architecture","description":"## Context
Existing users/workflows need a safe migration path from current capture/search behavior to the new recorder architecture.

## Scope
Create migration strategy covering data transition, compatibility mode, and cutover sequencing.

## Deliverables
- Migration phases (parallel run, validation, cutover, fallback)
- Data compatibility requirements between old and new paths
- Tooling/checklists for migration verification
- Backout plan preserving existing functionality

## Why this matters
A technically strong system still fails if migration is risky or opaque.

## Acceptance
- Migration steps are reproducible and reversible
- Compatibility assumptions are explicit and tested
- Operators have clear success/failure indicators during cutover","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.521204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.770614Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","migration","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.521204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.626822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.753758Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.770568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","title":"Define governance policy for retention, privacy, redaction, and privileged access","description":"## Context
Full transcript capture can include sensitive content. Governance policy (retention, access, redaction classes, audit expectations) must be explicit before wide enablement.

## Scope
Define security/privacy governance package for recorder data lifecycle and access.

## Deliverables
- Policy doc for retention classes, redaction taxonomy, and access tiers
- Compliance and data-handling rationale for stored transcripts
- Approval workflow for privileged access and exceptions
- Policy-to-implementation traceability map

## Why this matters
Governance is a hard prerequisite for responsible always-on logging.

## Acceptance
- Policies are concrete enough to implement and test
- Ownership/accountability for policy decisions is explicit
- Engineering controls are traceable to governance requirements","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T18:37:18.750048-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","policy","privacy","security","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.787277Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","title":"Define governance policy for retention, privacy, redaction, and privileged access","description":"## Context
Full transcript capture can include sensitive content. Governance policy (retention, access, redaction classes, audit expectations) must be explicit before wide enablement.

## Scope
Define security/privacy governance package for recorder data lifecycle and access.

## Deliverables
- Policy doc for retention classes, redaction taxonomy, and access tiers
- Compliance and data-handling rationale for stored transcripts
- Approval workflow for privileged access and exceptions
- Policy-to-implementation traceability map

## Why this matters
Governance is a hard prerequisite for responsible always-on logging.

## Acceptance
- Policies are concrete enough to implement and test
- Ownership/accountability for policy decisions is explicit
- Engineering controls are traceable to governance requirements","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T18:39:08.651245-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T18:39:08.651245-05:00","close_reason":"Governance policy: retention classes, sensitivity tiers, access control A0-A4, approval workflow, audit schema with hash chain, traceability matrix","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","policy","privacy","security","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.787277Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","title":"Create ops runbooks and alert strategy for recorder/index/search reliability","description":"## Context
Operations teams need practical runbooks for health monitoring, degradation handling, and incident escalation.

## Scope
Produce operational playbooks and alert strategy for recorder/index/search pipeline.

## Deliverables
- Runbook covering healthy-state checks and common failure signatures
- Alert definitions/severity mapping for lag/errors/corruption risks
- Escalation and ownership matrix
- Maintenance procedures (reindex, retention actions, failover)

## Why this matters
Operational clarity is required for confident long-term ownership.

## Acceptance
- Runbook enables on-call response without deep implementation context
- Alerts map clearly to actionable remediation steps
- Ownership and escalation paths are explicit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.56325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.838132Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.56325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.646322Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.82019Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.838082Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","title":"Define recorder-specific incident response and postmortem process","description":"## Context
Incidents in a recorder system are high-stakes due to forensic implications. Response and postmortem structure must be pre-defined.

## Scope
Define incident response and postmortem templates specific to recorder correctness, durability, and privacy failures.

## Deliverables
- Incident classification matrix (data loss, ordering drift, privacy leak, performance collapse)
- Standard response timeline/checklist
- Postmortem template with recorder-specific evidence requirements
- Feedback loop from incidents into bead backlog updates

## Why this matters
Structured incident handling shortens MTTR and preserves trust in recorder evidence.

## Acceptance
- Team can run a tabletop exercise using provided templates
- Evidence requirements are clear and feasible
- Postmortem outputs map to actionable follow-up work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.586906Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.663396Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","incident-response","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.586906Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.663351Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","title":"Produce adoption/onboarding/handoff package for long-term recorder ownership","description":"## Context
Sustained value requires explicit adoption and handoff: how contributors, operators, and agents use the system day-to-day.

## Scope
Create onboarding/handoff package that translates architecture into practical usage and maintenance rituals.

## Deliverables
- Role-specific onboarding guides (developer/operator/agent)
- Maintenance cadence recommendations (index health, retention review, quality checks)
- Knowledge-transfer checklist for future sessions/owners
- Success metrics for adoption (usage, query efficacy, incident outcomes)

## Why this matters
A self-documenting system only works if onboarding and handoff are engineered, not assumed.

## Acceptance
- New contributor can execute core workflows from docs alone
- Handoff checklist supports continuity across sessions
- Adoption metrics are defined and measurable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.607138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.717487Z","labels":["docs","epic","flight-recorder","handoff","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.607138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.682046Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.699663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.717443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
